steps.title: Locate the first median
steps.content: Data set A has $21$ values, so its median is the $11\text{th}$ value when the data are ordered.

steps.title: Remove the high value
steps.content: The value $19$ is above the middle of the ordered list. Removing it leaves the middle of the remaining $20$ values at the average of the original $10\text{th}$ and $11\text{th}$ values.

steps.title: Compare the medians
steps.content:
From the table, those two middle values make the same median as data set A, so removing the high erroneous value does not change the median.
The answer is B.
